claims.evidence_text: The N input of wheat ranged from 20-200kg N ha−1, and that of maize ranged from 30-400kg N ha−1.
claims.claim_summary: Observed N input to wheat across 569 observations ranged 20-200 kg N/ha.

claims.evidence_text: NUE of wheat ranged from 20 to 50kg kg−1 and NUE of maize from 20 to 80kg kg−1.
claims.claim_summary: Wheat nitrogen use efficiency (yield per unit N) ranged 20-50 kg grain per kg N (25th-75th percentile).

claims.evidence_text: WUE of wheat ranged from 0.5 to 1.5kg m−3 and WUE of maize ranged from 0.5 to 2.5kg m−3
claims.claim_summary: Wheat water use efficiency (yield per unit water) ranged 0.5-1.5 kg/m3 (25th-75th percentile).

claims.evidence_text: The water input of wheat ranged from 25 to 1000mm, and that of maize ranged from 150-2000mm. However, most of the observations were concentrated below 800mm.
claims.claim_summary: Seasonal water input (rainfall+irrigation) to wheat ranged 25-1000 mm, mostly below 800 mm.

claims.evidence_text: maize has a much lower planting density (1.5 to 18 plants m−2) than wheat (135 to 540 plants m−2)
claims.claim_summary: Typical wheat planting density is 135-540 plants per m2.

claims.evidence_text: straw mulching and plastic mulching increased yield, WUE and NUE of wheat by 20%
claims.claim_summary: Soil mulching (straw or plastic) increased wheat yield, WUE and NUE by ~20% on average.

claims.evidence_text: in North China Plain... evaporation during the full crop season averaged 28% of total evapotranspiration for winter wheat and 40% of that for summer maize
claims.claim_summary: For winter wheat in the North China Plain, soil evaporation averaged 28% of total seasonal evapotranspiration.
